description: Create Notebook 01: Data Foundation & Realistic Synthetic Generation. This is the most critical notebook - it generates 10,000 UMKM data points with REAL Jawa Barat geography (627 kecamatan across 27 kabupaten/kota) and NO circular logic. Features determine the target, not the other way around.
steps: Create the notebook as /projects/sandbox/geoumkm-smart-3/notebooks/01_Data_Foundation.ipynb using nbformat (nbformat.v4)

SECTION 1 - Geography Reference Data: Create a comprehensive dictionary of all 27 kabupaten/kota in Jawa Barat with their approximate centroid coordinates. The full list: Kab. Bandung (-6.98, 107.59), Kota Bandung (-6.91, 107.61), Kab. Bogor (-6.59, 106.79), Kota Bogor (-6.60, 106.80), Kab. Bekasi (-6.24, 107.00), Kota Bekasi (-6.24, 106.99), Kota Depok (-6.40, 106.82), Kab. Tasikmalaya (-7.35, 108.22), Kota Tasikmalaya (-7.33, 108.22), Kab. Cirebon (-6.71, 108.56), Kota Cirebon (-6.73, 108.55), Kab. Sukabumi (-6.92, 106.93), Kota Sukabumi (-6.92, 106.93), Kab. Garut (-7.22, 107.90), Kab. Ciamis (-7.33, 108.35), Kab. Kuningan (-6.97, 108.48), Kab. Majalengka (-6.84, 108.23), Kab. Sumedang (-6.86, 107.92), Kab. Indramayu (-6.33, 108.32), Kab. Subang (-6.57, 107.76), Kab. Purwakarta (-6.55, 107.43), Kab. Karawang (-6.32, 107.30), Kab. Pangandaran (-7.68, 108.65), Kab. Bandung Barat (-6.86, 107.46), Kota Cimahi (-6.87, 107.54), Kota Banjar (-7.37, 108.54)

SECTION 1 continued - Kecamatan Generation: For each kabupaten/kota, generate realistic kecamatan names. Use a curated list of real kecamatan names from each kabupaten (at minimum 15-30 per kabupaten for larger ones, 5-10 for kota). Generate coordinate offsets from kabupaten centroid (within 0.05-0.15 degrees). Target: ~627 total kecamatan entries.

SECTION 2 - Demographic Features (generated from geography): populasi (5000-500000, higher in urban areas near Bandung/Bekasi/Depok), kepadatan_penduduk (based on populasi and area), income_per_kapita (higher in Kota areas, Bandung metro, Bekasi-Depok corridor). Use log-normal distributions.

SECTION 3 - Infrastructure Features: jarak_ke_jalan_utama (km, lower in urban), jarak_ke_pasar (km), akses_internet_pct (higher in urban, 30-95%), skor_infrastruktur (composite 0-100). These correlate with urbanization level.
SECTION 4 - Competition Features: jumlah_kompetitor_radius_3km (based on kepadatan_penduduk, higher density = more competitors). Range 0-50.
SECTION 5 - Financial Access Features: jarak_ke_bank_terdekat (km, lower in urban), penetrasi_kur_pct (percentage of UMKM with KUR credit, varies 5-45%).

SECTION 6 - Risk Features: risiko_banjir (0-1, HIGHER for northern coastal: Indramayu, Karawang, Bekasi, Subang), risiko_gempa (0-1, HIGHER for southern: Garut, Tasikmalaya, Pangandaran, Ciamis, Sukabumi). Based on actual hazard zones.

SECTION 7 - Business Features: Generate 10,000 UMKM records. Each UMKM has: jenis_usaha (Makanan/Fashion/Kerajinan/Jasa/Pertanian with realistic proportions ~40%/20%/15%/15%/10%), tahun_berdiri (2010-2024), jumlah_karyawan (1-50, right-skewed), has_digital_presence (boolean, higher in urban), omset_bulanan (based on jenis_usaha, location quality, and age - NOT from score).

SECTION 8 - Target Variable Generation (NO CIRCULAR LOGIC): Generate is_survived_3yr (binary) as a function of features using a known logistic relationship. Higher survival probability when: good infrastructure + low competition + higher income area + not in high-risk zone + has digital presence + older business. Use a logistic function with known coefficients, add noise. Expected survival rate ~65-70%.

SECTION 9 - Location Potential Score (skor_potensi): Generate as a DERIVED metric using a weighted combination of normalized features (infra, demography, financial access, inverse risk, inverse competition). This is NOT a random number - it's computed from features. Range 0-100.

SECTION 10 - Save outputs: Save kecamatan reference data to data/kecamatan_reference.csv. Save full UMKM dataset to data/umkm_dataset.csv (and .parquet). Print summary statistics and data quality checks.
Include rich markdown cells explaining each section, the rationale for distributions chosen, and why the approach avoids circular logic.
acceptance_criteria: data/kecamatan_reference.csv contains ~627 rows with columns: kabupaten_kota, kecamatan, latitude, longitude, is_kota (boolean)
data/umkm_dataset.csv contains 10,000 rows with all required columns
No circular logic: skor_potensi is computed FROM features, is_survived_3yr is generated FROM features using a known relationship
Geographic coordinates are within Jawa Barat bounds (lat -7.8 to -6.0, lon 106.3 to 108.8)
Risk features correlate with actual geographic risk zones (northern = flood, southern = earthquake)
verification: Execute the notebook: jupyter nbconvert --to notebook --execute notebooks/01_Data_Foundation.ipynb --ExecutePreprocessor.timeout=300 --output executed_01.ipynb --output-dir /tmp/
Check outputs exist: python -c "import pandas as pd; df = pd.read_csv('data/umkm_dataset.csv'); print(f'Rows: {len(df)}, Cols: {len(df.columns)}'); assert len(df) == 10000"
Check no circular logic: python -c "import pandas as pd; df = pd.read_csv('data/umkm_dataset.csv'); print(df[['skor_potensi','omset_bulanan','risiko_banjir']].corr())"
